LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT RICHARD FRAIGE'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT OF MATTHEW T. KESSLER IS DENIED. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT SHOULD NOT HAVE BEEN FILED WITHOUT FIRST MAKING A MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE. HOWEVER, THE COURT SEES LITTLE POINT IN STRIKING THE COMPULSORY CROSS COMPLAINT AT THIS POINT. IF DEFENDANT WERE TO FILE A MOTION TO FILE THE CROSS-COMPLAINT, IT WOULD CERTAINLY BE GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN CSR #6185 (302/MJM)

REPLY TO DEFENDANTS OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFFS MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES RE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 111 AND 112 AND FOR PRODUCTION OF RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS (TRANSACTION ID # 55752856) FILED BY PLAINTIFF FRAIGE, RICHARD A NATURAL PERSON

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF RICHARD FRAIGE'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT MATTHEW KESSLERS FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 111 AND 112 AND FOR FURTHER PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS DENIED. THE TAX RETURNS ARE ALSO PRIVILEGED UNDER THE PRIVACY PRIVILEGE AFFORDED BY CAL. CONST. ART 1, SEC 1, AND COURT DOES NOT FIND FACTS SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT A WAIVER OF THE PRIVILEGE BY THE DEFENDANT. COUNSEL'S OFFER IN CORRESPONDENCE DOES NOT SUFFICE FOR A KNOWING WAIVER BY THE DEFENDANT. FURTHER, MOVING PARTY HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED SUFFICIENT NECESSITY TO OVERCOME THE PRIVILEGE. TAX RETURNS ARE NOT DISPOSITIVE ON THE ISSUE WHETHER UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW THE DEFENDANT WAS AN EMPLOYEE OR INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR. THE ISSUANCE OF W-2 FORMS OR 1099 FORMS BY THE PLAINTIFF WOULD BE IN THE CONTROL AND CUSTODY OF THE PLAINTIFF IN ANY EVENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE PRO TEM: NILS ROSENQUEST, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-28-2014 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Matthew Kesslers Further Responses To Requests For Production Nos. 111 And 112 And For Further Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Jul-25-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF RICHARD FRAIGE's Motion To Compel Defendant Matthew Kesslers Further Responses To Requests For Production Nos. 111 And 112 And For Further Production Of Documents is denied. The tax returns are also privileged under the privacy privilege afforded by Cal. Const. Art 1, Sec 1, and court does not find facts sufficient to support a waiver of the privilege by the defendant. Counsel's offer in correspondence does not suffice for a knowing waiver by the defendant. Further, moving party has not demonstrated sufficient necessity to overcome the privilege. Tax returns are not dispositive on the issue whether under California law the defendant was an employee or independent contractor. The issuance of W-2 forms or 1099 forms by the plaintiff would be in the control and custody of the plaintiff in any event. Prevailing party to submit order approved as to form. Judge Pro Tem: Nils Rosenquest, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT RICHARD FRAIGE'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT OF MATTHEW T. KESSLER.

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: denied. The cross-complaint should not have been filed without first making a motion for leave to file. However, the Court sees little point in striking the compulsory cross complaint at this point. If defendant were to file a motion to file the cross-complaint, it would certainly be granted. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR #6185 (302/MJM)
